Experience

Security Researcher

Independent — Remote

2024-01 – present

Conducting independent research at the intersection of machine learning and binary analysis. Developing novel approaches for automated reverse engineering using diffusion models and transformer architectures.

  • Built diffusion-based system for predicting masked x86-64 assembly spans
  • Trained models on 518K+ examples from system ELF binaries
  • Exploring publishable results for top ML/security venues

Reverse Engineer

Placeholder Corp — Austin, TX

2022-06 – 2023-12

Performed binary analysis and vulnerability research on embedded systems and firmware. Developed automated analysis tooling to accelerate reverse engineering workflows.

  • Analyzed firmware for IoT devices and embedded platforms
  • Developed automated binary analysis pipelines
  • Contributed to vulnerability disclosure processes

Education

B.S. Computer Science

University Placeholder — Austin, TX

2018-08 – 2022-05

Focus on systems programming, computer security, and machine learning.

  • Relevant coursework in OS internals, compilers, cryptography, and deep learning
  • Undergraduate research in binary analysis

Binary Patch Diffusion: Predicting Masked Assembly via Continuous Diffusion

Hunter A. Dyer — In preparation (2026)

A continuous diffusion approach for predicting masked spans of x86-64 assembly instructions, trained on system ELF binaries.
